cards.front: What does the 'super' keyword in Java refer to?
cards.back: The 'super' keyword in Java refers to the parent class of the current class.

cards.front: What is the purpose of a constructor in a Java class?
cards.back: The purpose of a constructor in a Java class is to initialize the objects of the class when an object is created.

cards.front: What is a multilevel hierarchy in object-oriented programming?
cards.back: A multilevel hierarchy in object-oriented programming is a class that inherits from another class, which in turn inherits from another class, and so on.

cards.front: What happens when a subclass has the same name as its superclass?
cards.back: When a subclass has the same name as its superclass, it is called method overriding, where the subclass provides a different implementation of the method.

cards.front: What is the difference between a constructor and a method in Java?
cards.back: A constructor in Java is used to initialize the objects of a class, whereas a method in Java is used to perform a specific action or operation.
